title: RayleaBot Plugin Manifest
description: Fixture-ready schema for plugin info.json. This file is the only formal source for install-time validation, compatibility gating, permission declaration, and migration boundary metadata.

id.description: Stable plugin identifier used by install validation, task tracing, permission grants, and plugin data paths.

name.description: Display name shown in management surfaces.

manifest_version.description: Manifest structure version. v0.1 is fixed to 1.

plugin_protocol_version.description: Plugin JSONL protocol version required by this package.

type.description: Plugin package form supported by v0.1.

runtime.description: Managed runtime family supported by v0.1.

entry.description: Relative plugin entry path inside the package root.

license.description: Open-source license identifier retained as required manifest metadata.

author.description: Single author identifier retained from the planning document. This contract does not introduce an authors array.

sdk_min_version.description: Minimum supported official SDK version, when the plugin depends on an official SDK helper layer.

runtime_version.description: Runtime minimum-version expression. This contract keeps the field as a narrow string.

data_schema_version.description: Plugin-private data schema version used to decide migration windows and automatic re-enable eligibility.

concurrency.description: Optional plugin event concurrency opt-in. When omitted, the platform treats the plugin as serial with concurrency 1.

platforms.description: Optional platform compatibility filter used during install-time gating.

role.description: Plugin semantic role used for management labeling and source-trust presentation.

icon.description: Optional relative asset path for the plugin icon inside the package root.

repo.description: Optional HTTPS repository URL for management-side source display.

homepage.description: Optional HTTPS homepage URL for management-side reference links.

screenshots.description: Optional management-side preview assets inside the plugin package.

system_dependencies.description: Optional external system prerequisites declared for operator visibility only.

capabilities.description: Platform capabilities the plugin intends to use. The current formal capability set includes message.send, message.reply, logger.write, storage.kv, storage.file, http.request, config.read, config.write, scheduler.create, event.expose_webhook, render.image, and plugin.list.

permissions.description: Permission declaration used for first install grants, upgrade re-grant decisions, and scope review.

required.description: Capabilities that must be explicitly granted before the plugin may be enabled.

optional.description: Capabilities that stay disabled by default and may be granted later.

scopes.description: Scope boundaries attached to permission grants.

http_hosts.description: Allowed outbound HTTP hosts for the http.request capability.

storage_roots.description: Allowed storage roots for storage.file. v0.1 only freezes plugin_data.

webhooks.description: Controlled webhook route declarations used with event.expose_webhook.

default_config.description: Optional initial non-sensitive plugin config written into the plugin namespace on first enable only.

dependencies.description: Language-level package dependencies only. Plugin-to-plugin dependencies are intentionally out of scope for v0.1.

require_install_scripts.description: Explicit high-risk install-path declaration for packages that require npm lifecycle scripts or local build steps.

commands.description: Declared command list for command parser directed delivery and help generation.

name.description: Command name matched after stripping the configured command prefix.

aliases.description: Alternative command names that route to the same handler.

description.description: Short command description for help output.

permission.description: Minimum chat-side permission level required to invoke this command. When omitted, the platform falls back to auth.default_level.

route.description: Controlled short route segment without slashes, dot-dot, or query strings.

$comment: The manifest stays focused on install-time validation, permission declaration, management role labeling, source metadata, default plugin config seeding, migration boundaries, and opt-in event concurrency. The current formal capability set includes message.send, message.reply, logger.write, storage.kv, storage.file, http.request, config.read, config.write, scheduler.create, event.expose_webhook, render.image, and plugin.list. Binary runtime support remains outside this frozen surface.
